domain: F degrees of freedom above 1E5 (measured accuracy envelope; the both-large orientation binds, crossing near df 1.5E5-3E5)
cause: incomplete-beta continued fraction loses accuracy while its local convergence test still passes
observed_effect: without the guard, errors grow to ~4E-7 by df~1E9; the public F surface now REJECTS df > 1E5 with CVErr(xlErrNum)
notes: Strict validated-domain policy (PROB_F_ValidateEnvelope). F_Density is closed-form and unrestricted. Distinct from the LogBeta normalization defect.

domain: upper tail beyond the central region (standardized z above ~2.75-3.25)
cause: the standard-normal survival core loses RELATIVE accuracy in the deep tail (absolute error stays ~1E-17)
observed_effect: relative error grows monotonically: ~1E-14 at z=3, ~1E-13 at z=4, ~5E-11 at z=5, ~5E-10 at z=6
notes: Tight central contracts are domain-restricted accordingly. Still far better than 1-CDF cancellation; only the RELATIVE bound degrades, not the absolute.
